headline: DISCOVERY SILVER HITS 770 g/t GOLD OVER 0.3M AT PORCUPINE TVZ — 143 Holes Confirm Zone Growth
snippet: Discovery Silver Corp. (TSX: DSV) reported assays from 143 holes totalling 44,740 metres at its Porcupine Operations, with the TVZ zone returning 72.46 g/t gold over 3.8 metres including a 770 g/t gold intercept over 0.3 metres — confirming high-grade growth potential at the project's advanced-stage expansion target. Results span the Hoyle Pond, Borden, and Pamour mines alongside the Dome and TVZ growth projects.

headline: NEW FOUND GOLD PUSHES DROPKICK TO 1.4 KM STRIKE — 24.8 g/t Au over 14m From 324m Depth
snippet: New Found Gold Corp. extended the Dropkick Zone at its Queensway Gold Project in Newfoundland to over 1.4 kilometres along strike and 300 metres depth, with highlights from 2025 drilling including 24.8 g/t gold over 14 metres from 324 metres. The high-grade domain remains open in multiple directions, with resource drilling resuming in Q2 2026 ahead of an updated mineral resource estimate.

headline: F3 URANIUM EXTENDS TETRA ZONE IN ATHABASCA STEP-OUTS — Confirms Structural Link to High-Grade JR Zone
snippet: F3 Uranium Corp. intersected uranium mineralization in three of six step-out holes at the Tetra Zone on its Patterson Lake North Project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin, extending the zone's strike and confirming a structural connection to the high-grade JR Zone. Uranium spot was trading near $86.90/lb on April 22 — up 32% year-over-year — as AI data centre buildout and utility contracting activity underpin long-term demand.

headline: SILVER INSTITUTE: SIXTH STRAIGHT SUPPLY DEFICIT IN 2026 — 46.3 Million Oz Shortfall as Investment Demand Holds
snippet: The Silver Institute's 2026 World Silver Survey projects a 46.3 million ounce supply deficit — the sixth consecutive year of structural shortfall — as mine output stays flat while investment and industrial demand from solar panel manufacturing remain elevated. Since 2021 the market has drawn down a cumulative 762 million troy ounces from above-ground stocks to cover the gap.

headline: GOLDMAN HOLDS $12,650/T, TRAXYS TARGETS $15,000 — China Acid Ban Splits Copper Forecasters
snippet: Goldman Sachs held its 2026 copper average forecast at $12,650/tonne despite projecting a 490,000-tonne surplus, while Traxys CEO Mark Kristoff called for $15,000/tonne within two to three years on structural electrification demand. China's looming May 1 ban on sulphuric acid exports — used in copper leaching operations — adds fresh supply risk, with 45% of DRC copper output directly exposed.

headline: RAMP METALS DRILLS 21m OF 0.80% Cu AND 17 g/t Ag AT ROTTENSTONE SW — VMS Zone Found in Every Hole
snippet: Ramp Metals Inc. (TSX-V: RAMP) drilled 0.80% Cu, 17.01 g/t Ag, 0.09 g/t Au, and 0.73% Zn over 21 metres in its first winter hole at Rottenstone SW in Saskatchewan, with VMS-style mineralization now confirmed in every hole drilled along the 1,100-metre Rush anomaly. Higher-grade intervals include 1.50% Cu and 24.15 g/t Ag over 3 metres.

headline: TALISKER TRIPLES BRALORNE DRILL PLAN TO 105,000 METRES — British Columbia Gold Project Gets Full Commitment
snippet: Talisker Resources expanded its Bralorne Gold Project drill program in British Columbia from 30,000 metres to 105,000 metres — a 3.5x increase — with 83,000 metres for resource conversion at Mustang, Bralorne West, and Olympus, plus 22,000 metres of exploration targeting the historic Congress vein system 15 kilometres north of camp. The scale of expansion signals strong management confidence in the project's near-mine and exploration upside.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE CONSOLIDATES FINLAND IN THREE SIMULTANEOUS DEALS — Rupert, Aurion and B2Gold JV Stake, 500K oz Target
snippet: Agnico Eagle simultaneously agreed to acquire Rupert Resources and Aurion Resources and take full ownership of B2Gold's Fingold JV stake, consolidating 2,492 square kilometres across Finland's Central Lapland Greenstone Belt. The three-deal sweep targets a 500,000-oz/year regional gold production hub within a decade, with up to US$500 million in projected development and operating synergies.

headline: CLSA RAISES 2026 GOLD TO $4,840, SEES $5,500 IN THE MEDIUM TERM — Producers Still at P/NAV Discount
snippet: In Sprott's weekly Gold SWOT review, CLSA upgraded its gold price forecasts to $4,840/oz for 2026, $5,130 for 2027, and $5,500 for 2028, driven by persistent central bank accumulation and structural safe-haven demand. Gold producers are currently trading at a 3% discount to historical P/NAV averages — pointing to an equity opportunity as miners work to close their performance gap with the metal itself.
